pairs.question: What is the full name of the provincial government department abbreviated as SSWB in Newfoundland and Labrador?
pairs.answer: The full name of the department abbreviated as SSWB is the Ministry of Social Supports and Well-Being.

pairs.question: Who appoints the head of the Ministry of Social Supports and Well-Being in Newfoundland and Labrador?
pairs.answer: The head of the Ministry of Social Supports and Well-Being is appointed by the Premier and formally sworn in by the Lieutenant-Governor of Newfoundland and Labrador.

pairs.question: What were the key responsibilities of the Child and Youth Services division under SSWB?
pairs.answer: The Child and Youth Services division was responsible for child protection services, foster care, adoption, kinship care, youth services, youth corrections programs, disability policy, adult protection, and seniors advocacy.

pairs.question: How did the Ministry of Social Supports and Well-Being originate before becoming a standalone department?
pairs.answer: Before becoming a standalone department in April 2009, many of its functions were part of a branch within the Department of Health and Community Services.

pairs.question: When and why was the Ministry of Social Supports and Well-Being renamed?
pairs.answer: The ministry was renamed to the Department of Children, Seniors and Social Development in 2016 as part of a reconfiguration of government departments.

pairs.question: What percentage of children in Newfoundland and Labrador were receiving services from Child and Youth Services (CSSD) in 2016 according to the NL Auditor General?
pairs.answer: In November 2016, the NL Auditor General reported that 6,252 children — eight percent of the children in Newfoundland and Labrador — were being served by CSSD programs.

pairs.question: What notable organizational change occurred in 2021 regarding income support services in Newfoundland and Labrador?
pairs.answer: In 2021, the income support division of the Department of Immigration, Skills and Labour was relocated into the Child and Youth Services division of the Ministry of Social Supports and Well-Being.

pairs.question: Can you list the ministers who have led the department since 2025?
pairs.answer: As of October 29, 2025, the ministers who have led the department are Jamie Korab (appointed May 9, 2025) and Joedy Wall (appointed October 29, 2025).
